candidate_problems.text: Determine whether any criterion of identity can be justified as explanatorily privileged across legal, historical, biological, and artifact cases, contrary to the output's context-sensitive pluralism.
Specify how to decide when two contexts are genuinely tracking different abstract objects versus merely disagreeing about the same one.
Test whether the same under-specification diagnosis applies to personal identity cases, where moral and phenomenological stakes may resist the ship-style reframing.
